name: AWS WAF GeoMatch Country Allow/Deny Baseline
objective: Provide a deployable baseline for geographic request filtering using AWS WAF GeoMatch with explicit rollout safety (COUNT first, then BLOCK).
risk_mitigated: Reduces exposure to abusive or unauthorized traffic from countries where the application has no business requirement, and supports compliance-driven traffic restrictions.
deployment_notes: Start with COUNT action for at least 7-14 days to establish legitimate country traffic patterns.
Review sampled requests and WAF logs grouped by country before moving to BLOCK.
If adopting deny-list mode, initially include only countries with clear abuse patterns.
If adopting allow-list mode, validate multinational user journeys, CDN egress behavior, and third-party integrations first.
Promote to BLOCK in phased environments (dev -> staging -> production) with rollback prepared.
tuning_notes: Multinational applications should prefer deny-list mode to avoid accidental customer lockout.
Allow-list mode is best for region-locked services or strict compliance boundaries.
Confirm geolocation behavior for VPN/mobile carrier traffic and satellite offices.
Account for synthetic monitoring, CI/CD probes, and partner APIs that may originate outside primary countries.
Keep an exception process (temporary country allowance or scoped path exceptions) for business continuity.
monitoring_guidance: Track allowed/count/blocked requests by country code and URI path.
Alert on sudden spikes from newly blocked countries to detect evasion or misclassification.
Monitor conversion/login/support metrics for regions after enabling BLOCK to catch business impact quickly.

pack.default_action_guidance: Use COUNT initially; switch to BLOCK only after traffic validation.

rules.name: geo-denylist-count-baseline
rules.notes: Starter deny-list example. Replace country_codes with environment-specific risk/compliance list.

rules.name: geo-allowlist-count-baseline
rules.notes: Starter allow-list example (count traffic outside approved countries). Do not enable together with deny-list in the same ACL without intentional logic design.

terraform_examples.description: Attach one GeoMatch rule variant to an existing aws_wafv2_web_acl. Keep explicit priority and visibility metric names.

safety.rollback: Revert action from BLOCK to COUNT or disable the geo rule while preserving metrics for analysis.
prerequisites: WAF logging or sampled requests enabled
Business-approved country policy and exception owner
